title: Home Is Where The Heart Attack Is

jokes.text: I heard the doctor say he thinks Uncle Phil might have Dunlap syndrome... Mean his belly done lapped over his belt.
jokes.setup: Will mentions a medical-sounding 'Dunlap syndrome' that sounds serious
jokes.punchline: Reveals it's a play on 'done lapped' - his belly has overlapped his belt
jokes.explanation: Classic Will wordplay - sets up fake medical condition then delivers clever pun. Well-structured and character-specific.

jokes.text: Hold on a minute. I'll have you know my father is not fat. He's just big-boned. Larger than life.
jokes.setup: Carlton defensively lists increasingly absurd euphemisms for Phil's weight
jokes.punchline: The escalating denial culminates in 'larger than life'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton character moment - earnest denial with escalating euphemisms shows his loyalty and naivety.

jokes.text: Yeah, he's also larger than the Volvo.
jokes.setup: Carlton's defensive 'larger than life' comment
jokes.punchline: Will deflates it with a concrete, absurd comparison to their car
jokes.explanation: Sharp comeback that uses specific detail (Volvo) to puncture Carlton's grandiose defense. Great economy.

jokes.text: Cholesterol is gathering in his arteries... stopping the blood flow like rush-hour gridlock. Hold it, Dr. Quinn, Medicine Woman.
jokes.setup: Carlton gives overly technical medical explanation
jokes.punchline: Will mocks him with pop culture reference to TV medical drama
jokes.explanation: Good character interplay - Carlton being overly studious, Will deflating with pop culture. Reference feels a bit easy.

jokes.text: Dad will probably outlive all of us. Only if he eats us first.
jokes.setup: Carlton confidently states his dad will outlive everyone
jokes.punchline: Will twists it into a cannibalism joke about Phil's appetite
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark twist on Carlton's optimism. Perfect economy and unexpectedly subversive for family sitcom.

jokes.text: Oh, did I tell you? I went in on a racehorse.
jokes.setup: Context of discussing Phil's health and responsible lifestyle choices
jokes.punchline: Phil casually mentions gambling on horses as if it's responsible
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's blind spots about responsibility - treats gambling as casual investment. Subtle character moment.

jokes.text: I mean, hey, I'm getting tired of the fat jokes myself. What is wrong with you people? I weigh the same thing I weighed in high school. Yeah, if you add up all four years.
jokes.setup: Phil defensively claims he weighs the same as high school
jokes.punchline: Will reveals the twist - adding up all four years of high school weight
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - lets Phil seem reasonable then delivers devastating mathematical punchline. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: Guess I'm not getting as tired of those fat jokes as I thought, huh?
jokes.setup: Will just delivered a particularly harsh fat joke
jokes.punchline: Will acknowledges his own contradiction about being 'tired' of fat jokes
jokes.explanation: Smart meta moment where Will catches himself contradicting his own stated position. Good self-awareness.

jokes.text: Where are you going? Shooting the rapids at Dead Man's Falls... But Trevor died doing a bungee jump. I know. I'm working up to that.
jokes.setup: Hilary explains dangerous activity as therapy for Trevor's death
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's doing completely different dangerous activity as 'practice'
jokes.explanation: Peak Hilary logic - doing dangerous activity A to prepare for dangerous activity B. Perfectly captures her character.

jokes.text: Whoa, whoa, Hil. That's not your parachute. That's my book bag. That would've been embarrassing.
jokes.setup: Hilary is about to leave for skydiving
jokes.punchline: She's grabbed Will's book bag instead of parachute, calls it 'embarrassing'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary moment - potential death is just 'embarrassing'. Visual gag enhances character-driven humor.

jokes.text: I'm supposed to imagine myself 20 years from now. No wonder you're so depressed.
jokes.setup: Carlton explains school assignment about future
jokes.punchline: Will suggests imagining Carlton's future would depress anyone
jokes.explanation: Standard Will-Carlton dynamic. Good economy but fairly predictable insult.

jokes.text: You are little Carlton.
jokes.setup: Carlton talks about seeing 'little Carlton' in the future
jokes.punchline: Will points out Carlton already is little Carlton
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan observation. Great economy - three words deflate Carlton's entire fantasy.

jokes.text: Your wife? Oh, this is science fiction.
jokes.setup: Carlton mentions having a wife in his future scenario
jokes.punchline: Will suggests Carlton getting married is pure fantasy
jokes.explanation: Another standard Will insult about Carlton's romantic prospects. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: You probably don't recognize it without a thick coating of batter and gravy.
jokes.setup: Phil asks what the healthy food is
jokes.punchline: Geoffrey explains Phil only knows chicken when it's heavily processed
jokes.explanation: Good Geoffrey moment - dry observation about Phil's eating habits. Well-crafted setup/punchline.

jokes.text: I can't give up my favorite foods cold turkey with... Oh, my God. Turkey. With pillowy mounds of mashed potatoes...
jokes.setup: Phil uses expression 'cold turkey' about giving up food
jokes.punchline: The word 'turkey' triggers food fantasy escalation
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that triggers Phil's food obsession. Good character integration and escalating fantasy.

jokes.text: Put your hands up. Step away from the chips.
jokes.setup: Ashley catches Phil and Geoffrey with unhealthy snacks
jokes.punchline: She treats it like a police bust
jokes.explanation: Good visual gag treating food policing like law enforcement. Ashley taking control is character-appropriate.

jokes.text: And doing a wonderful job, I might add. Shut up, Geoffrey.
jokes.setup: Geoffrey compliments Ashley's food policing
jokes.punchline: Ashley immediately shuts him down
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat showing Ashley's no-nonsense attitude. Good economy but fairly standard.

jokes.text: Ashley hid the remote control, so I have to walk to the TV.
jokes.setup: Phil explaining why this situation is 'crazy'
jokes.punchline: The terrible hardship is having to walk to the television
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Phil's privileged perspective. Walking to TV is genuine hardship to him.

jokes.text: You know, I'd give anything for a cheeseburger, Will. Anything. Why, I might even finance your little trip to Mexico.
jokes.setup: Phil desperately wants a cheeseburger
jokes.punchline: Reveals he'd pay for Will's expensive trip just for food help
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's desperation through escalating offers. Good character work and plot advancement.

jokes.text: Ashley has spies at every fast food restaurant for miles.
jokes.setup: Phil's diet restrictions are getting out of hand
jokes.punchline: Ashley has created a surveillance network
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Ashley's diet enforcement. Spy network is a fun absurd image.

jokes.text: Well, I'll make a New Year's resolution, and by March... February... Well, you have my solemn vow. And that's this New Year's, right?
jokes.setup: Phil promises to start diet by New Year's, keeps shortening timeline
jokes.punchline: Will has to clarify which New Year's he means
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phil's procrastination. Will's clarifying question adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: My therapist was right. You just aren't nurturing enough.
jokes.setup: Family expresses concern about Hilary's dangerous activities
jokes.punchline: She interprets safety concerns as lack of nurturing support
jokes.explanation: Perfect Hilary logic - twists genuine concern into criticism of family. Great character integration.

jokes.text: Doesn't my word mean anything to you? Of course it does, darling. Better call the Chinese restaurants too.
jokes.setup: Phil asks if his word means anything
jokes.punchline: Vivian says yes while simultaneously planning more food restrictions
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction between what Vivian says and does. Shows she knows Phil too well to trust him.

jokes.text: You got the stuff? Right here. You got the money? You take a check?
jokes.setup: Phil and Will conduct food transaction like drug deal
jokes.punchline: Phil asks if drug dealer accepts checks
jokes.explanation: Good parody of drug deal tropes. Phil asking about checks breaks the illusion perfectly.

jokes.text: It's good to see you're not wasting time chewing.
jokes.setup: Phil is eating extremely fast
jokes.punchline: Will observes Phil's inhaling food without chewing
jokes.explanation: Simple observational humor about Phil's desperate eating. Functional but not particularly creative.

jokes.text: Relieves cramps, bloating and irritability. Oh, no, no, this is Geoffrey's.
jokes.setup: Will offers Phil medicine for stomach problems
jokes.punchline: Phil realizes it's feminine hygiene product
jokes.explanation: Fairly cheap gender-based humor. The Geoffrey twist adds mild character element but overall weak.

jokes.text: Don't worry, he's my fourth heart attack today... and I already lost one of them. A two-death day is rare.
jokes.setup: Will asks if Phil will be okay
jokes.punchline: EMT casually discusses death statistics like baseball scores
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny professional detachment. EMT treating deaths like statistics creates uncomfortable humor.

jokes.text: Could be because you're leaning on his air hose.
jokes.setup: Will complains Uncle Phil can't breathe
jokes.punchline: EMT points out Will is causing the problem
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup. Good economy but fairly standard medical emergency humor.

jokes.text: I'm staying with you till the end. That was a really poor choice of words. I'm sorry, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Will tries to be supportive during medical emergency
jokes.punchline: Realizes he implied Phil might die
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment where Will catches his own poor phrasing. Shows his genuine care despite awkwardness.

jokes.text: We gonna go for plenty rides in ambulances, man... you gonna be all right, Uncle Phil.
jokes.setup: Will tries to reassure Phil about future
jokes.punchline: Accidentally implies many more medical emergencies
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's nervousness through poor word choice. Character-driven humor in tense situation.

jokes.text: Well, if I say he is and he croaks, you're gonna be upset, right? You're a real people person, aren't you? I guess.
jokes.setup: EMT explains why he won't reassure Will
jokes.punchline: Will sarcastically comments on bedside manner, EMT agrees obliviously
jokes.explanation: Good character work with oblivious EMT. Dark humor balanced with Will's sarcastic reaction.

jokes.text: Hey, if he still wants to stop for a burger... I can get Troy to swing by the drive-through. With the siren on, we don't have to wait in line.
jokes.setup: EMT offers to help with Phil's food cravings
jokes.punchline: Suggests using emergency vehicle for fast food convenience
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd suggestion that shows EMT's complete professional detachment. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I hate to interrupt this guilt-fest... but I think I have to take the rap for this one.
jokes.setup: Family is blaming themselves for Phil's heart attack
jokes.punchline: Will interrupts the mutual guilt to claim responsibility
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's honesty. 'Guilt-fest' is nice economical description.

jokes.text: I mean, the fried rice had bean sprouts in it.
jokes.setup: Will admits to sneaking Phil unhealthy food
jokes.punchline: Tries to justify it by mentioning token healthy ingredient
jokes.explanation: Good character detail showing Will's attempts at rationalization. Realistic guilty behavior.

jokes.text: Uncle Phil, what are you doing there? I had a heart attack, Will. Hey, how come you didn't tell me you weren't my uncle, man?
jokes.setup: Will thinks he's talking to Uncle Phil
jokes.punchline: Discovers it's a random patient who played along
jokes.explanation: Excellent mistaken identity gag with absurd patient who craves attention. Well-structured revelation.

jokes.text: Well, nobody ever comes to visit me. I crave the attention.
jokes.setup: Patient explains why he pretended to be Will's uncle
jokes.punchline: Reveals lonely desperation for any human connection
jokes.explanation: Sad but funny explanation that adds depth to the gag. Touches on real issue of elderly isolation.

jokes.text: Will, see if he's gonna finish his IV.
jokes.setup: Patient continues the charade
jokes.punchline: Asks about sharing medical equipment like it's food
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd continuation. Treating IV like leftover food is perfectly weird hospital logic.

jokes.text: Yeah, I got a son. He don't come to visit me either.
jokes.setup: Discussion about Carlton not visiting Phil
jokes.punchline: Patient reveals he also has absent son
jokes.explanation: Adds depth to patient character while maintaining the running gag about his loneliness.

jokes.text: Not Kevorkian. Kevarkian with an A.
jokes.setup: Doctor introduces himself
jokes.punchline: Corrects pronunciation to avoid association with Dr. Kevorkian (assisted suicide)
jokes.explanation: Smart topical reference. Doctor's correction acknowledges the unfortunate name similarity.

jokes.text: Carlton's a little bigger, wears a bow tie.
jokes.setup: Doctor asks if baby is Carlton
jokes.punchline: Will describes Carlton like he's just a larger infant
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton description that infantilizes him while being technically accurate. Great character insight.

jokes.text: Don't be stupid, Carlton. What are you doing? Cleaning the oven. What's your problem? It says right here on the label to let foam sit for 45 minutes. My hands are tied.
jokes.setup: Will asks why Carlton isn't at hospital
jokes.punchline: Carlton uses oven cleaning as excuse to avoid emotional situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Carlton behavior - using literal rule-following to avoid emotional confrontation. Excellent character work.

jokes.text: You come near me, I'll spray.
jokes.setup: Will threatens to force Carlton to hospital
jokes.punchline: Carlton threatens him with oven cleaner like it's mace
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Carlton's avoidance. Treating cleaning product as weapon shows his desperation.

jokes.text: You don't understand, Will. Yes, I do. You're selfish.
jokes.setup: Carlton tries to explain his avoidance
jokes.punchline: Will cuts through with blunt assessment
jokes.explanation: Will's directness cuts through Carlton's deflection. More dramatic than comedic but effective.

jokes.text: Call me Russell. I'm on break now. You wanna go downstairs and have an early breakfast?
jokes.setup: Doctor offers to help grieving Hilary
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks her on breakfast date
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate timing for romantic advance. Shows doctor's poor bedside manner but fairly standard humor.

jokes.text: Oh, look at that, I'm being beeped. I didn't hear any beep.
jokes.setup: Doctor tries to escape Hilary's TMI about therapy
jokes.punchline: Hilary calls out his obvious lie
jokes.explanation: Standard awkward escape attempt with Hilary being obliviously direct. Functional but predictable.

jokes.text: Yeah... cheesecake is my kryptonite.
jokes.setup: Carlton says Phil is like Superman to him
jokes.punchline: Phil admits his weakness using Superman metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback that maintains the Superman metaphor while addressing Phil's real problem. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Hey, Superman, ask junior to get me a bedpan.
jokes.setup: Patient overhears Superman conversation
jokes.punchline: Continues to insert himself using the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Maintains patient's character consistency while extending the Superman callback. Good persistence.

jokes.text: Hey, Uncle Phil, bet you never thought... you'd get a standing O in this house?
jokes.setup: Family welcomes Phil home with standing ovation
jokes.punchline: 'Standing O' works as both ovation and reference to his health condition
jokes.explanation: Clever double meaning that works on multiple levels. Good wordplay that fits the moment.

jokes.text: You was probably worried about just seeing this kitchen again, right? I'm sorry, that's a leftover fat joke. I'm tripping.
jokes.setup: Will makes another fat joke
jokes.punchline: Immediately acknowledges and apologizes for the habitual behavior
jokes.explanation: Good self-awareness showing character growth. 'Leftover fat joke' is clever phrasing.

jokes.text: I'll cover all bets on that one.
jokes.setup: Phil promises to become 'lean, mean judicial machine'
jokes.punchline: Will expresses skepticism in gambling terms
jokes.explanation: Standard skeptical response. Good economy but fairly predictable given Phil's track record.

jokes.text: Oh, by the way, I dented the Mercedes.
jokes.setup: Ashley promises not to upset Phil anymore
jokes.punchline: Immediately mentions damaging his expensive car
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and contradiction. Ashley's immediate confession after promising not to upset him.

jokes.text: And if you ever do anything to scare me like that again, I'll kill you.
jokes.setup: Vivian expresses relief that Phil is safe
jokes.punchline: Threatens to kill him if he scares her again
jokes.explanation: Classic parental contradiction - loving concern expressed as threat. Good irony and character truth.

jokes.text: Oh, man, we don't have no remote. Sure we do. Geoffrey!
jokes.setup: Will mentions missing remote control
jokes.punchline: Phil reveals Geoffrey is the human remote
jokes.explanation: Perfect ending that shows Phil hasn't really changed. Geoffrey as human remote is classic character dynamic.

tmdb_overview: Will sneaks greasy, high-fat foods to Philip after he promises to diet and exercise, then blames himself when Philip has a heart attack.
